Title: Understanding the mechanism of red light-induced melatonin biosynthesis facilitates the engineering of melatonin-enriched tomatoes

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi; dual-luciferase assay; luciferase complementation imaging; protein degradation assay with MG132; western blot; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression in developing fruit is highly consistent with melatonin accumulation trend according to MMN database
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Activated under red light supplementation; facilitates SlPIF4 degradation through the 26S proteasome pathway and thereby releases repression of SlCOMT2. Silencing abolishes red-light inducibility of melatonin accumulation.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant; growth promotion; delayed senescence; improved fruit nutritional quality and shelf life; human health-promoting indoleamine
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlPHYB2 is a positive upstream light-signaling component for melatonin accumulation in tomato fruit. 2. SlPHYB2 interacts with SlPIF4 and promotes its 26S proteasome-dependent degradation under red light. 3. RNAi of SlPHYB2 reduces SlCOMT2 expression, lowers melatonin content, and eliminates red-light induction.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating the SlPHYB2-SlPIF4-SlCOMT2 module can enhance melatonin in fruit, though direct phytochrome manipulation may affect broader light signaling.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Red light supplementation (30% red light, 657 nm)
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes red light-induced melatonin biosynthesis by relieving SlPIF4 repression of SlCOMT2
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome-based coexpression/coregulation analysis using MMN and Tomato Expression Atlas databases
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Fruit developmental expression correlated with melatonin according to MMN

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Tryptophan decarboxylase; pyridoxal-dependent aromatic L-amino acid decarboxylase family
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; VIGS in tomato fruit; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Selected from fruit transcriptome correlation/expression analysis as a fruit-relevant candidate
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ for transient overexpression; pTRV for VIGS
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as the enzyme for the first step from tryptophan to tryptamine in tomato melatonin biosynthesis; not the main red-light regulated step discussed later.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant; growth promotion; delayed senescence; improved nutritional quality
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlTDC2 catalyzes the first step of tomato melatonin biosynthesis from tryptophan to tryptamine. 2. Functional screening by transient expression and silencing supported its contribution to melatonin production. 3. It serves as an upstream structural component rather than the red-light-responsive control point emphasized in this study.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could contribute to precursor supply for melatonin engineering, but was not highlighted as the preferred engineering target in this paper.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports melatonin biosynthesis by providing tryptamine precursor

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; VIGS in tomato fruit; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Selected from fruit transcriptome correlation/expression analysis as a fruit-relevant candidate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes melatonin biosynthesis by converting tryptamine to serotonin
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for pathway reconstruction and precursor supply, but not the prioritized engineering target in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant; growth promotion; delayed senescence; health-promoting metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlT5H was identified as the enzyme for converting tryptamine to serotonin in tomato. 2. Functional screening supported its role in melatonin biosynthesis. 3. It operates upstream of the alternative downstream branches.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression and RNAi in tomato; transient overexpression/VIGS; in vitro enzyme assay with recombinant protein; metabolite profiling; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Two downstream branches linking serotonin or 5-methoxytryptamine to melatonin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases melatonin accumulation; catalyzes N-acetylserotonin production from serotonin and can also catalyze melatonin formation from 5-methoxytryptamine
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable structural gene for boosting melatonin, but red-light-responsive engineering focus in this study was SlCOMT2 promoter editing rather than SlSNAT.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant; growth promotion; delayed senescence; health-promoting metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetylates serotonin to N-acetylserotonin and can also acetylate 5-methoxytryptamine to melatonin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlSNAT is a bona fide tomato melatonin biosynthetic enzyme. 2. It functions in two possible routes by producing N-acetylserotonin from serotonin and melatonin from 5-methoxytryptamine. 3. Overexpression increases and RNAi decreases fruit melatonin levels.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor and can directly form target product in alternative route

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression and RNAi in tomato; transient overexpression/VIGS; in vitro enzyme assay with recombinant protein; metabolite profiling; expression analysis; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mainly expressed in fruits after the breaker stage; induced by red light supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes melatonin accumulation by catalyzing conversion of N-acetylserotonin to melatonin; required for red light-induced melatonin increase
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key enzyme in N-acetylserotonin route under red light context
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Red light supplementation (30% red light, 657 nm)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Good candidate for increasing fruit melatonin, especially in combination with red-light responsive modules.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant; growth promotion; delayed senescence; health-promoting metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlASMT5 is strongly expressed in ripening fruit and promotes melatonin accumulation. 2. Recombinant SlASMT5 converts N-acetylserotonin to melatonin but not serotonin to 5-methoxytryptamine. 3. RNAi of SlASMT5 reduces red light-induced melatonin accumulation, indicating participation in the red-light-responsive N-acetylserotonin route.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression and RNAi in tomato; transient overexpression/VIGS; in vitro enzyme assay with recombinant protein; metabolite profiling; expression analysis; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mainly expressed in fruits after the breaker stage; induced by red light, but not required for red-light responsiveness of melatonin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes melatonin accumulation through the 5-methoxytryptamine route by converting serotonin to 5-methoxytryptamine
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Red light supplementation showed this branch is not the main induced route
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be used to exploit the alternative branch of melatonin biosynthesis, though it is less relevant for red-light-based engineering in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant; growth promotion; delayed senescence; health-promoting metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates serotonin to form 5-methoxytryptamine and does not convert N-acetylserotonin to melatonin in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlASMT7 functions in the alternative 5-methoxytryptamine route of melatonin biosynthesis. 2. Recombinant SlASMT7 converts serotonin to 5-methoxytryptamine but not N-acetylserotonin to melatonin. 3. RNAi specifically reduces 5-methoxytryptamine levels, and SlASMT7 is dispensable for the red-light-induced increase in melatonin.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor in alternative route

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression and RNAi in tomato; transient overexpression/VIGS; in vitro enzyme assay with recombinant protein; expression analysis; subcellular localization; dual-luciferase assay; Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; promoter editing; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mainly expressed in fruits after the breaker stage; induced by red light supplementation; upregulated in RNAi-SlPIF4 and downregulated in RNAi-SlPHYB2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes melatonin accumulation by converting N-acetylserotonin to melatonin; central target of red-light signaling and promoter editing
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Promoter DNA methylation is high at green stage and likely suppresses expression before ripening, based on unpublished database cited in Discussion
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for red light-induced melatonin biosynthesis in fruit
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Red light supplementation (30% red light, 657 nm)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target; editing the SlPIF4-binding motif in the SlCOMT2 promoter produced stronger melatonin enhancement than SlPIF4 knockout while preserving stage specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant; growth promotion; delayed senescence; improved fruit nutrition and shelf life; human health-promoting metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates N-acetylserotonin to form melatonin; does not convert serotonin to 5-methoxytryptamine in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promoter editing is preferred over SlPIF4 knockout because global SlPIF4 disruption may perturb multiple signaling pathways
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlCOMT2 is a core melatonin biosynthetic enzyme in the N-acetylserotonin route and a major determinant of fruit melatonin levels. 2. Red light enhances melatonin accumulation largely through induction/de-repression of SlCOMT2. 3. SlPIF4 directly binds the P2 G-box in the SlCOMT2 promoter to repress expression, and editing this binding site dramatically elevates fruit melatonin.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mainly in ripening fruit after breaker stage

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Opposite developmental trend to SlPHYB2 according to MMN; active in fruit and functions during ripening-stage regulation
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome/coexpression information from MMN used for context
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of SlCOMT2; self-activation on its own promoter; protein destabilization by SlPHYB2 via 26S proteasome under red light
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; dual-luciferase in tobacco leaves and tomato protoplasts; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; RNAi; CRISPR knockout; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates melatonin biosynthesis; silencing/knockout increases fruit melatonin
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Direct knockout is less desirable for breeding because SlPIF4 is a master regulator with vital functions in multiple signaling pathways
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for overexpression constructs; native SlCOMT2 promoter target site edited by CRISPR
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major negative regulator linking red light signaling to melatonin biosynthesis through direct repression of SlCOMT2
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Autoregulation supported: SlPIF4 binds its own promoter for self-activation; this self-activation is inhibited by SlPHYB2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly represses SlCOMT2 promoter activity; no binding detected to SlASMT5 promoter despite predicted G-box
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlPIF4 directly binds the P2 G-box of the SlCOMT2 promoter and represses its transcription. 2. RNAi or CRISPR knockout of SlPIF4 increases melatonin accumulation in tomato fruit. 3. Red light acts upstream through SlPHYB2 to promote SlPIF4 degradation, thereby releasing SlCOMT2 expression.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Melatonin biosynthesis, specifically branch control via SlCOMT2 expression
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Editing the SlPIF4-binding site in the SlCOMT2 promoter is more effective than knocking out SlPIF4 for increasing melatonin under normal growth conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant; growth promotion; delayed senescence; improved fruit nutrition and health benefits
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functions in ripening fruit; RNAi elevates SlCOMT2 expression and melatonin under normal and red-light conditions
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promoter editing to uncouple SlPIF4 repression of SlCOMT2 is a superior strategy to direct SlPIF4 knockout.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with SlPHYB2 in vivo; no stable transcriptional complex beyond this was defined
